🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-16 08:42 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 5 items need immediate escalation (🚨), 6 more need management attention (🔴)
Urgent vs smaller5 urgent, 6 need a nudge, 13 to watch, 3 handled well
Total open (active, excl. triage)27 -4 vs last digest (31→27); 2 new, 6 resolved
4
🚨 Escalate now
6
🔴 Needs attention
14
🟡 Watch
3
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14303 — P1 all-brands CRM login outage (WorkOS 3rd-party auth) - instant ack, root cause in ~1min, comms out in 18min, still partially recovering
  • INC-14301 — P3 Star Casino NL bonus-distributor slow processing, lead assigned, ~33min old - too fresh to flag

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-14163 — clean - g2 SSRF/no-consumers queue, root cause found and partner informed within ~15min, confirmed fixed same hour
  • INC-14152 — clean - SBTSolutions clone-lifecycle type fix, partner confirmed resolution
  • INC-13931 — clean - G2 Welcome-Offer shortfall traced to partner-side event delay, full 193-payout portfolio audit shared proactively
  • INC-14098 — ⚠ closed - Shuffle CF-WAF fix confirmed, but the CTO's own partner-facing comms draft (personally chased 12:17 CEST 15 Jul) still had no confirmation it was ever sent before closure (C9/C11)
  • INC-13893 — ⚠ closed - Tuohi LC39 missed bonuses; closed after ~36h of silence with the earlier wrong 'scaling' explanation to the partner never corrected (C10/C11)
  • INC-12436 — Andrea: Tuohi is under ICU observation and all its incidents are handled by a dedicated task force - closed deliberately as part of that process, not dropped.
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 4

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open · evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

~300 duplicate bonus grants/week continuing after 50+ days; the accepted interim workaround (REW-176, per management note 16 Jul) is parked until mid-Aug by Rewards capacity, and the BetConstruct root-cause path has zero traction (A2/A4/B5). The open decision is scheduling/risk, not partner sign-off.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50+ days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. BetConstruct account-manager gap means zero external traction since 5 Jun. Rewards team's own Urgent ticket (REW-176) remains blocked by capacity until mid-Aug. Re-checked: 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts (conversation still closed since 06 Jul), incident.io metadata completely unchanged - situation fully static, financial leakage continuing unabated.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50+ days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • A3: minimizing language contradicts confirmed weekly leakage
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed (since 06 Jul) implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • 503-as-success workaround (May) - rejected by partner 28 May
  • PR #122 idempotency fix - closed unmerged
  • PR #123 observability only - merged, no fix
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note 16 Jul), opened Urgent 9 Jul, blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug
Timeline (5)
  • 26 May incident opened, root cause confirmed same day
  • 29 May H2Bet rejects proposed 503-as-success workaround
  • 18 Jun root-cause refresh confirms no technical mitigation deployed, PR #122 closed unmerged
  • since 18 Jun no further incident.io updates
  • 15/16 Jul re-checks: 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts, Intercom conversation still shows closed while incident.io stays open P1 - no movement whatsoever

→ Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue, or formally document risk-acceptance of the leakage until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager gap to leadership.

INC-13895 slackP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 06:10 UTC
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401 @ /external/pusher/:brand true)

Nothing has moved since the morning re-chase: the partner is silent, the recurrence hasn't triggered escalation, and two bot close-nudges have gone unanswered - stalled in a way that risks a silent auto-close on a still-unconfirmed fix, on the 2nd occurrence of the same fault in a week.

Fusion API 401 errors blocked on-site notifications/Rich Inbox for Spinbet Terragon - the 2nd occurrence of this alert family in a week (see INC-12617). Partner was proactively notified once ~4h9m after the alert but it went unanswered a day; Joachim re-chased the morning of 15 Jul. Since then: total silence - no partner reply, no re-chase, no escalation of the recurrence - only an administrative lead reassignment.

Open breaches
  • B5: bot's silent-close nudge from 14 Jul still unanswered, and a second auto-close nudge fired 20:22 CEST 15 Jul with no response either
  • A4: 2nd Fusion API 401 incident in a week (see INC-12617), still not escalated to the owning team
  • C9: partner completely silent for over a day since the re-chase, no further follow-up sent
Proposed / pending
  • Partner asked to check/confirm the API exchange and 401 cause on their end - no response received
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • will update when more information available - still no follow-up sent
Timeline (4)
  • Morning 15 Jul Joachim re-chases the partner
  • No partner reply since
  • 20:22 CEST 15 Jul bot fires a second 'mark as resolved?' nudge, also unanswered
  • 16 Jul 08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned Nazareno Scibilia -> Joachim Farrugia (administrative only, no new substantive action)

→ Escalate the recurring Fusion API 401 pattern to the owning team now (2nd time in a week), send one more direct partner follow-up with a deadline, and respond to the bot's resolve prompts rather than letting it auto-close.

INC-11079 slackP2open · evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager

This crossed from aging silence into an active, currently-firing recurrence: the exact same alert fired again today with a real backlog, got auto-merged with zero human commentary, and nobody re-chased the partner or escalated despite them admitting a week ago they have no fix and no ETA - concrete, present-tense evidence of continued customer harm handled entirely by automation.

Segurobet Consent Manager queue stalled since 27 Jun due to partner's (BetConstruct/Betco) Operator API 500s. Partner admitted 9 Jul they're 'at a loss', no ETA ever given. New this cycle: the underlying alert fired again live on 15 Jul (10:45 UTC, backlog of 3 messages), auto-creating a new incident that Matteo silently merged into this one at 12:46 CEST with zero commentary, no re-chase to the partner, no escalation - concrete, current proof the partner still hasn't fixed anything. Effectively the 4th segurobet/BetConstruct reliability incident in ~5 weeks.

Open breaches
  • C9: 6+ day silence after partner admitted uncertainty, now compounded by a fresh live recurrence with still no re-chase
  • B5: no formal escalation to account/partner management despite 18+ days duration and a brand-new active recurrence today
  • C10: underlying alert auto-resolved/auto-merged with no human verification
  • A4: 4th partner-reliability incident in ~5 weeks, zero follow-ups tracked, silently absorbed via auto-merge
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
  • no FT-side workaround proposed
  • zero incident.io follow-ups exist
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given
Timeline (3)
  • 15 Jul 10:45 UTC alert fires again live (backlog of 3 messages)
  • 15 Jul 12:46 CEST auto-created incident merged into this one by Matteo, no narrative attached
  • No partner re-chase, no account-management escalation, no incident.io update posted alongside the merge

→ Escalate now to account/partner management - the partner has been stuck 18+ days with an admitted lack of a fix, and the problem just recurred live today. Get a human to comment on the merge, re-chase the partner directly, and consider a formal reliability/account escalation.

INC-5630 slackP3open · evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC
Coinbar SMS Issues with Credit

77+ days since report, zero forward progress since 29 May's one substantive question went unanswered, lead has rotated through at least 6 people, and new alerts keep auto-merging into an effectively unattended incident - a chronic multi-month failure with no vendor-management escalation track and no escalation in sight, regardless of its P3 label.

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Root cause identified almost immediately, but 2.5+ months later there is zero forward progress. Re-checked: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io metadata fully unchanged - confirmed nothing has moved since 29 May besides automated alert noise.

Open breaches
  • A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track - now 48+ days of pure automated churn since the last substantive question
  • D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May) never answered or tracked - lead rotated to at least a 6th person, new alerts keep merging in unattended
Timeline (5)
  • 30 Apr incident opened, 5 brand queues found with zero consumers, 1M+ messages backlogged
  • external SMS provider outage identified as root cause almost immediately
  • 29 May one substantive question raised in-channel - never answered or tracked
  • since 29 May pure automated churn, lead rotated to at least a 6th person
  • 15/16 Jul re-checks: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io unchanged - still zero decisive action

→ Escalate to vendor/commercial management for the external SMS provider outage rather than continuing to passively wait; assign one single accountable owner instead of rotating leads; answer the outstanding 29 May question and decide whether to purge/reroute the 1M+ message backlog.

🔴 Needs attention 6

INC-13917 slack intercomP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 01:21 UTC
Campeon: Slow processing on credit service (from lifecycles)

Technically stable (mitigation holding, no new delay reports, conversation unsnoozed with nothing new to report), but the incident.io record has not moved since creation and is now at risk of being auto-resolved in a state that doesn't reflect the mitigation or actual root cause.

Campeon lifecycle coin-crediting delayed up to 2h on 11 Jul, root cause auto-claim + serialized credit path. An 8xlarge instance upgrade completed as short-term mitigation (~13:35 CEST 15 Jul); partner was told and confirmed. Since then: no further partner reports and no new Slack activity beyond the bot's routine auto-close nudge (03:21 CEST 16 Jul) - the Intercom conversation auto-unsnoozed itself this morning (09:01 CEST) with no new human reply either side - but incident.io has never been updated at all, still shows 'Investigating' with zero status updates despite the mitigation and partner comms having already happened.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io still shows zero status updates and 'Investigating' despite completed mitigation, lead handoff, and partner comms from over a day ago - the bot's auto-close nudge is now live against that inaccurate record
  • C11: partner told a generic 'performance bottleneck'; internally-confirmed root cause still not disclosed
Proposed / pending
  • Temporary: scale credit-service compute to 8xlarge - completed
  • Permanent: credit VC directly vs auto-claim bonus - undecided
  • Add audit trail for Rewards bonus-config edits - raised, not tracked
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T11:18
  • Rewards team confirmed a performance-bottleneck backlog - partial/generic explanation
Timeline (5)
  • ~13:35 CEST 15 Jul 8xlarge instance upgrade completed
  • Linus informs partner via Intercom of the infra upgrade, then confirms completion
  • Partner sends one more delay example just before the fix lands
  • 03:21 CEST 16 Jul bot fires 'no activity - mark as resolved?' nudge, unanswered
  • 16 Jul 09:01 CEST Intercom conversation auto-unsnoozed ('until tomorrow' from 15 Jul) - no new human message either side since

→ Post the overdue incident.io status update (mitigation applied) before responding to the bot's resolve prompt, and reconcile the partner-facing explanation with the internal RCA.

INC-13738 slackP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 04:45 UTC
Betiro - No unsubs since April 2026

Real forward progress this cycle (fix + the specifically-requested monitor both landed), so no longer a silent-drift situation - but two-plus real breaches remain: a 3-month player opt-out failure across gambling brands still has no legal/compliance escalation, and 3 newly-flagged brands are unaddressed.

IP-whitelisting change from ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected ~3 months. Fix merged 13 Jul. After Andrea's direct 15 Jul push, Simon Brown's PR #196 (daily unsubscribe-events monitor) merged 16:28 CEST - the monitor Andrea specifically asked for has landed, unchanged since (0 new messages). Still open: no legal/compliance escalation for the ~3-month opt-out failure, no confirmed retroactive remediation, and 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed.

Open breaches
  • D12: still no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
  • C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes
  • Partner threads for 2/3 known brands winding down while 3 newly-flagged brands remain unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • Monitor landed (PR #196) - addresses detection gap
  • Open: retroactive remediation plan
  • Open: legal/compliance escalation
  • Open: extend investigation to Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino
Timeline (5)
  • 07 Apr unsubscribe flow silently broken
  • 13 Jul incident opened, fix merged same day
  • 15 Jul 16:28 CEST PR #196 (unsub-events monitor) merged following Andrea's direct push
  • 16 Jul re-check: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io unchanged - no further movement on legal escalation or the 3 new brands
  • 16 Jul 06:45 CEST bot fires an auto-resolve nudge, unanswered - legal/compliance escalation and the 3 new brands remain unaddressed

→ Escalate the opt-out failure to legal/compliance given regulatory exposure; confirm/execute retroactive remediation; open tracking for the 3 newly-flagged brands before treating this as closed.

INC-12617 slackP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC

Unchanged in substance, but notable for what did NOT happen: an already multi-breach incident with a known recurrence pattern had zero activity of any kind this cycle - not even a bot nudge - drifting further out of view.

Fusion API 401 errors; root cause ruled out as FT-side, partner informed via Teams DM 07 Jul and never re-chased since. No new Slack activity and no incident.io change at all this cycle - now roughly 9 days of total silence. The exact 401 pattern has recurred only once more, as INC-13895 (checked separately) - no 3rd occurrence yet.

Open breaches
  • C9: no partner re-chase in 9 days
  • C10: alert cleared nearly a week ago, never formally closed
  • B5: silent-close warnings and manual escalation both ignored
  • A4: recurring Fusion API 401 pattern (2nd occurrence INC-13895) still not escalated to the owning team

  • log investigation, rule out FT-side config - done
  • inform partner - done once, never re-chased
Partner view

  • last partner message unanswered

  • incident.io unchanged: status 'Waiting on Partner', updated_at still 10 Jul 12:05:12Z

→ Escalate to the owning API team about the recurring 401 pattern (2 confirmed occurrences); re-chase the partner directly since the original Teams DM has had zero response in 9 days; formally close or get a resolution confirmation.

INC-13664 slack intercomP3open · evidence to 15 Jul 23:15 UTC
[P3] Intercom Incident : SBTSolutions Production

This is a genuine comms-integrity problem, not just paperwork lag - the partner currently has an inaccurate account of what happened, and the incident is one auto-nudge away from being silently closed with that inaccurate account never corrected.

SBTSolutions reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing; Matteo migrated the messaging system to Kafka without partner sign-off, then told the partner it would be scheduled - misrepresenting work already done. Matteo said in Slack 'this can be pretty much closed', but incident.io is still Waiting on Partner and no resolution message has gone to the partner. New: the bot posted a stale-incident resolve-nudge at 01:15 CEST 16 Jul, unanswered.

Open breaches
  • D15: stuck 'Waiting on Partner' since 13 Jul, now compounded by an unanswered auto-resolve nudge
  • C11: partner told the migration would be scheduled when it had already been silently applied
  • C10: no resolution/closing message sent to the partner despite the fix being considered done internally
Proposed / pending
  • Migrate to Kafka (root fix) - implemented, considered effective, still not formally confirmed with partner or in incident.io
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-11T18:40
  • Kafka migration during low-traffic window, no partner action needed - told after already applied; fix considered holding, not yet confirmed to partner
Timeline (1)
  • 16 Jul 01:15 CEST incident.io auto-resolve nudge posted, no response yet

→ Before closing, Matteo (or a manager) must send the partner an accurate resolution message (migration completed, not 'scheduled') and only then move incident.io to Closed.

INC-12179 slackP3open · evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts

This is the textbook long-runner-with-no-action-plan case the age-based re-check exists for - a confirmed root cause and a ranked, cheap fix list have sat completely untouched for 12 days on a recurring failure class (3rd occurrence).

llm-agent tool execution failing on Daytona sandbox cold-resume. Root cause confirmed 04 Jul; 4 ranked follow-ups filed same day. Directly re-verified via follow_up_list: all 4 fixes remain 'outstanding' 12 days later, including the ranked real fix and the zero-code immediate mitigation. No Slack activity this cycle; incident.io untouched since 04 Jul.

Open breaches
  • A4: 3rd occurrence of this recurring failure class - all 4 ranked follow-ups confirmed still outstanding 12 days after RCA, zero assignee movement
  • D14/D15: incident.io stuck 'Investigating' since 04 Jul (12 days), bot resolve-nudge unanswered, zero new activity of any kind
Proposed / pending
  • probe toolbox data-plane (real fix) - outstanding
  • raise DAYTONA_EXEC_MAX_ATTEMPTS (band-aid) - outstanding
  • user-facing transient-error copy - outstanding
  • reduce cold-resume churn - outstanding
Timeline (3)
  • follow_up_list re-checked 16 Jul: all 4 follow-ups still status=outstanding with no movement
  • Zero new Slack messages
  • incident.io status/updated_at unchanged since 04 Jul

→ Escalate to Jeremy/AI Team lead directly (a bot nudge alone hasn't worked) to get at minimum the immediate env-only mitigation actioned this week, and assign an owner+date for the real fix before this recurs a 4th time.

INC-10902 slackP4open · evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
H2Bet incidents - night - DO NOT CLOSE

This matches the 'recurring issue heading for silent close' pattern the guidelines call out directly - a P4 bucket incident can persist indefinitely under 'DO NOT CLOSE', and the actual schema fix still has no owner or date attached.

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup, consolidated into this 'DO NOT CLOSE' bucket since 26 Jun. A separate log-volume config bug was fixed, but the producer/consumer payload type-mismatch itself is still unfixed. The promised 'close Monday 13 Jul if stable' checkpoint has now passed by 3 days with no update logged, and there was no new Slack activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug identified 26 Jun, still unfixed ~20 days later, with 2 more recurrences merged in as late as 12 Jul - a recurring issue with no resolution plan
  • D15: incident.io stale - no substantive update since 09 Jul, the promised 13 Jul close-if-stable checkpoint has now passed by 3 days unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • Schema/type-mismatch fix - still not implemented, only the reload-loop/log-volume side-fix has landed
Timeline (2)
  • No new Slack messages
  • incident.io updated_at ticked to 14 Jul 13:29 but visible status-update text unchanged, still dated 09 Jul

→ Simon Brown to either commit to a concrete date for the producer/consumer schema fix (not just further monitoring) or formally acknowledge the missed 13 Jul checkpoint with a revised one.

🟡 Watch 14

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open · evidence to 14 Jul 12:42 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Management note 16 Jul: solved as an incident; remaining work is the impacted-player cross-check with Estelarbet — FT re-ran the impact analysis and asked the partner for complainant IDs on 15 Jul (confirmed in Intercom). Downgraded 🚨→🟡 per note; watch for the partner's reply and final report delivery (C10 loop closure).

Cloudflare-origin security check blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traffic on 2 Jul; mitigated within ~2h, root-cause fix merged same evening. Partner (Estelarbet/PLIN) has been waiting 13+ days for a promised RCA report, through what is now a confirmed 3rd broken promise: the 15 Jul promised date fully passed with only a clarifying question sent (13:17 UTC) and zero further activity since - the report itself still has not been delivered.

Open breaches
  • C10: impact cross-check + final report pending — awaiting partner's complainant list (requested 15 Jul)
  • D15: incident.io status timestamps misrepresent when fix landed
  • A4: same CF/unsub-blocking pattern recurring across 3 incidents (also see INC-13738)
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • file-serving revert - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13, 14, 15 Jul), still not delivered - 15 Jul's promised date has now fully passed unmet
Timeline (7)
  • 02 Jul fixes merged same day
  • 06 Jul status moved Investigating->Fixing 4 days late
  • 10 Jul status -> Monitoring (unchanged since)
  • 13-14 Jul two broken 'update tomorrow' promises, next promised 15 Jul
  • 15 Jul 13:17 UTC Giancarlo sends partner a clarifying question (re-running impact analysis), not the promised report
  • 16 Jul 08:35 CEST Andrea reassigns the Intercom ticket and pauses its SLA (admin action, no new partner-facing content)
  • 16 Jul re-check: 0 new Slack messages, no new human Intercom reply - the 15 Jul promised RCA date has now fully lapsed with nothing delivered, a confirmed 3rd broken promise

→ Chase Estelarbet (Gloria) for the complainant user IDs if no reply by 17 Jul; deliver the cross-checked impact report to close the loop.

INC-11707 slack intercomP1open · evidence to 15 Jul 12:54 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Betpipo Production

No news is good news - quiet since the fix and partner-facing explanation, but only about half a day into the ~2-day window, so not confirmed stable yet.

Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; root cause confirmed as a shared UserTokens table collision. All 4 fix PRs merged and a custom release deployed to Betpipo/Betoffice at 14:51 CEST 15 Jul. Matteo informed the partner via Intercom at 14:33 UTC with root cause and fix explanation. Since then: no new Slack or Intercom activity - over 12 hours of quiet with no further logout reports, consistent with the planned ~2-day observation window before closing.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status is 'Waiting on Partner' since the fix went out - reasonable, ~12h into the planned 2-day silence window, a good sign but not yet confirmed stable
Proposed / pending
  • scope token invalidation to IsAdminToken (4 PRs) - all merged and deployed
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-26T09:08
  • multiple 'stabilized' claims (01,03,06 Jul) all contradicted by recurrence
Timeline (3)
  • 14:51 CEST 15 Jul custom release deployed to Betpipo/Betoffice
  • 14:33 UTC 15 Jul Matteo explains root cause/fix to partner via Intercom
  • No new activity since

→ No action needed beyond continuing to watch for partner reports over the remaining ~1.5 days before closing; the previously-flagged 'reply to partner' action is already done.

INC-13916 slack intercomP1open 47h· evidence to 15 Jul 20:07 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : BrazilBet Production

private — review directly

INC-14151 slack intercomP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 06:52 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : MeridianBet Production

Joachim followed through on the morning plan and sent the partner re-check request via Intercom at 08:39 UTC - the planned action happened. Remaining: partner confirmation of both reported failures (C10) and an incident.io update with the real root cause — the logged update still describes the superseded FE fix (D15).

MeridianBet couldn't add sports bonuses in a Peru onboarding flow. Matteo matched it to a prior incident and got an existing FE fix deployed, asking the partner to retest - but the partner reported the fix still failed, and a second user reported a related failure on a different action. Hours later, Hikmet found a genuinely different root cause (a hidden required AuthType field with no default) and fixed it directly. Joachim followed through on the morning plan and asked the partner via Intercom to re-check; awaiting their reply.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io's logged update still describes the superseded FE fix; real root cause (hidden required AuthType, setup-side) not yet posted
  • C10: partner re-check requested 08:39 UTC, awaiting reply; second user's report still needs explicit confirmation it's covered by the same cause
Proposed / pending
  • Original (failed) FE fix from prior incident's branch - deployed, partner found it didn't work
  • Actual fix (Hikmet): remove 'required' flag from AuthType field - applied, unverified with partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T11:58
  • last partner message unanswered
  • First deployed fix - reported by partner as not working
  • Escalation to high priority tomorrow AM if not fixed - superseded by an unannounced second fix
Timeline (8)
  • 14:43 UTC first FE fix deployed, partner asked to retest
  • 14:51 UTC partner reports hard refresh still fails; separately a second user reports failure on a related action
  • 15:49 UTC Matteo tells partner FE still investigating, will escalate tomorrow AM if not fixed by EOD
  • 19:34 UTC Hikmet finds the real root cause (AuthType field required+hidden, no default) and removes the required flag, confirms he can save - never logged to incident.io or told to partner
  • 21:45 CEST and 02:03 CEST 16 Jul bot auto-close nudges fire, both unanswered, latest asking directly if it can be marked resolved
  • 16 Jul 08:36-08:38 CEST: Joachim asks about deployment; Hikmet: no deployment needed (setup fix), 'we can ask the partner directly and close this'; Joachim taking it to the partner
  • 16 Jul 08:52 CEST: Matteo acknowledges root cause; suggests more verbose field-validation errors (follow-up candidate)
  • 16 Jul 08:39 UTC Joachim asks partner (Jamie) via Intercom to re-check if the issue is resolved - no reply yet

→ Await partner reply to the 08:39 UTC re-check; once confirmed, post the real root cause to incident.io before closing; ticket Matteo's verbose-validation-errors suggestion as a follow-up (A2).

INC-13757 slackP2open · evidence to 16 Jul 04:21 UTC
Campeon: Incorrect games mappings

Real, substantive engineering progress happened and was properly written up in incident.io - two of three resolver bugs fixed and the immediate customer-impact case manually remediated. The one remaining gap is purely administrative: the Lead field never updated to reflect the de facto handover to Sam.

Significant progress since last check. Samuela root-caused why ~120 of the ~3,545 brand-wide mismatched game mappings survived the reconciliation endpoint: three resolver failure modes. Two ('&'-naming and non-ASCII title stripping) are root-caused, fixed, tested, and deploying tomorrow AM. The third (alias-shadowed games) is understood but not yet fixed - needs a quick owner decision, doesn't currently affect active challenges. The specific customer-impacting cases were manually corrected in the DB so player challenge-credit is no longer blocked. incident.io was substantively updated (17:02 UTC) with the full narrative.

Open breaches
  • B8/A1: incident.io Incident Lead still shows Daniel Lindengren (on vacation) despite Samuela having driven 100% of the visible engineering work
Proposed / pending
  • Deploy Mode B/C fixes tomorrow AM, re-run dry run
  • Decide Mode A approach after a quick call with games/greco owners
Timeline (6)
  • 15 Jul 18:02-18:04 CEST Sam confirms ~120 residual games needing manual review
  • 15 Jul 18:18 CEST Sam manually corrects the two challenge-referenced mappings, unblocking player challenge-credit
  • 15 Jul 18:58 CEST Sam posts full root-cause writeup: three resolver failure modes
  • 15 Jul 19:02 CEST incident.io formally updated with the full narrative
  • Mode B/C fixes committed, deploying tomorrow AM with a fresh dry-run planned
  • 16 Jul 06:21 CEST bot fires an auto-resolve nudge, unanswered (fixes still pending tomorrow AM deploy, correctly not yet closed)

→ Update incident.io Incident Lead to Samuela Lundborg to reflect reality; otherwise on track.

INC-12998 slackP2open · evidence to 14 Jul 10:56 UTC
frank-casino: deployment ping-pong is stuck on CrashLoopBackOff
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: the deployment ping-pong service is not critical — a crash-loop here is not a critical-path failure (B7 does not apply). Watch-level, not escalate.

Downgraded 🚨→🟡 per management note 16 Jul: ping-pong is a non-critical service, so the crash-loop is not a B7 critical-path failure. Still worth watching: the incident has no active owner in the channel, a blank incident.io summary (D15), and ~6 days without a diagnostic — it should be fixed at normal priority or closed deliberately, not left drifting.

frank-casino deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff, unchanged again since last check: 0 new Slack messages, incident.io still 'Investigating', summary still blank. Lead (Giancarlo Laferla) still assigned, still not posting; incident now 6.4+ days old with zero visible progress.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io summary field is blank; no diagnostic ever posted
  • A1: no one actively owns it — both leads have left the channel
Timeline (5)
  • 09 Jul alert fires, lead auto-paged, leaves channel same evening
  • 10 Jul lead reassigned to Giancarlo ~20h after report
  • 12 Jul bot nudge unanswered
  • 14 Jul current lead leaves channel, incident still open
  • 16 Jul re-checked again - 0 new Slack messages, incident.io metadata unchanged, now 6.4+ days stale with no evidence anyone is actively working it

→ Assign at normal priority: fix the crash-loop or make a deliberate decision (e.g. suppress the alert / decommission) and close with a one-line rationale.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open · evidence to 15 Jul 14:37 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Partner engagement remains good and stable, but the concrete long-term fix commitment still has no tracked owner or due date - same gap as last run, now directly confirmed via follow_up_list.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags, blocking new-player journeys. Partner (Anna Koller) agreed to a weekly manual re-sync as interim mitigation, confirmed GiG never shipped a fix on their end. No new activity since last check. follow_up_list confirms zero follow-ups exist, so the update_epoch fix remains untracked despite the partner actively waiting on it.

Open breaches
  • C10: update_epoch fix recommended twice, still zero tracked incident.io follow-up despite partner actively engaged and waiting on it - confirmed via follow_up_list
Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • GiG source-side tag fix - partial, ONT/119 still pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key - proposed twice, zero incident.io follow-ups tracking it
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • evaluate update_epoch reordering fix - in progress, no ticket yet
Timeline (1)
  • follow_up_list(INC-10045) returns 0 follow-ups - confirms nothing has been created for update_epoch

→ Create a tracked incident.io follow-up for the update_epoch ordering-key fix with an owner and due date - still outstanding.

INC-9820 slackP2open · evidence to 14 Jul 16:01 UTC
RDS OOM all brands - gradually increasing overtime

Steady, no regressions; only the resilience-hardening follow-up still lacks an owner as the incident heads toward closure.

Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, fixed via PR #416, rolled out fleet-wide by 9 Jul. On 14 Jul the lead closed remaining gaps: the capping follow-up is completed, and a new RAM/swap-alarm follow-up was created and assigned. follow_up_list confirms only the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up remains outstanding and still unassigned.

Open breaches
  • A4: one follow-up (activity-manager-go resilience) remains outstanding and unassigned as closure nears
Proposed / pending
  • Completed: cap performance_schema memory (PR #416)
  • In progress: RDS/Aurora memory & swap alarm thresholds (assigned)
  • Outstanding, unassigned: bounded retry/backoff for activity-manager-go on transient MySQL OOM errors
Timeline (1)
  • follow_up_list(INC-9820) confirms: capping follow-up completed; RAM/swap alarm follow-up outstanding, assigned to Simon Brown/SRE; activity-manager-go resilience follow-up outstanding, unassigned

→ Assign an owner to the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up before this incident is closed out.

INC-14168 slack intercomP2open 19h· evidence to 16 Jul 07:52 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Rhino Production

Downgraded 🔴→🟡: lead reassigned and escalated correctly this morning, root cause found within an hour of proper escalation, and the partner explicitly said the earlier silence was fine given the low urgency - the C10 breach is resolved. Remaining gap is just posting the root cause/fix status back to incident.io and the partner.

Rhino reported via Intercom that the Save button stays disabled when editing unsubscribe-page HTML. Lead reassigned to Joachim this morning, who escalated to the front-end subteam; Carl Bondin found the likely root cause within ~50 minutes (new template validation blocking script tags). Separately, the partner (Dario) told Fast Track directly that the lack of a reply yesterday was fine since this isn't P1/P2 - the unanswered-urgency-question breach is resolved.

Open breaches
  • C11/D15: root cause found but not yet posted to incident.io or communicated to partner
Proposed / pending
  • Front-end investigating script-tag template validation as root cause - in progress, no fix shipped yet
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T14:06
  • Told tech team likely won't respond until next business day (15:13 UTC)
  • Front-end root cause found 16 Jul - not yet communicated to partner or fixed
Timeline (6)
  • 15 Jul 14:06 UTC Rhino reports the bug via Intercom bot
  • 15 Jul 15:12-15:13 UTC Fábio reproduces, asks partner urgency (unanswered at the time)
  • 16 Jul 05:53-08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned James -> Nazareno -> Joachim Farrugia
  • 16 Jul 08:14 CEST Joachim escalates to front-end subteam, notes Partner Support also reproduced it
  • 16 Jul 09:03-09:04 CEST Carl Bondin (front-end) finds likely root cause: script tags now blocked in the template
  • 16 Jul 07:40 UTC partner (Dario) tells Fast Track it's fine there was no reply yesterday since it's not P1/P2

→ Post the script-tag root cause to incident.io and give the partner a fix ETA once front-end confirms the deploy plan.

INC-14177 slackP2open 18h· evidence to 16 Jul 06:25 UTC
campeon: no consumers on queue campeon.actions.callbackconsumer (messages: 1, in: 0/s)

One documentation gap (D15) plus the original ~1h43m quiet gap (B5) on a very low-impact (1 message), still-young P2; lead reassigned twice since, now Joachim actively engaging with a partner push planned today - not yet sent.

A single stuck message on the campeon callback-consumer queue triggered a P2 alert; James acknowledged instantly but the incident sat in Triage ~1h43m before moving to Investigating, then jumped straight to Waiting on Partner because a colleague (Gian) relayed the partner message over Teams since James lacks Teams access. No content/timestamp of that partner message is captured in the incident record. Lead reassigned to Joachim Farrugia this morning, who is actively engaging and plans a partner push today.

Open breaches
  • D15: record not self-contained - partner comms happened off-platform via a colleague with no content/timestamp captured
  • B5: ~1h43m quiet gap between alert and investigation start, no visible activity logged in between
Proposed / pending
  • Escalate to partner via Teams (delegated to Gian) - done, but undocumented in-channel
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Partner message relayed via Teams by Gian on James's behalf - content/timestamp not captured in the incident channel
  • Joachim: partner push planned this morning (16 Jul) - not yet sent as of 10:42 CEST
Timeline (6)
  • 15 Jul 16:44 UTC alert fires (1 message stuck), James acknowledges within seconds
  • 15 Jul 18:27 UTC (~1h43m later) incident accepted, Triage -> Investigating
  • 15 Jul 18:28 UTC moved to Waiting on Partner; partner message relayed via Teams by a colleague on James's behalf
  • 16 Jul 05:54 CEST Incident Lead reassigned James Hodgson -> Nazareno Scibilia
  • 16 Jul 08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned again, Nazareno -> Joachim Farrugia
  • 16 Jul 08:24 CEST Joachim: 'Waiting on them' (partner); 'Will send a push this morning'
INC-12754 slackP3open · evidence to 15 Jul 13:34 UTC
Campeon Nexto unable to login

Fix is solid and no recurrence has been reported, but the incident has drifted a full extra work-week without anyone formally closing or re-confirming it - the silent-drift pattern this age-check targets. Low urgency but easy to fix.

Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch. Root cause found and workaround delivered within ~15 minutes; Patrik personally cleared Cloudflare cache and restarted crm-api same day (08 Jul), fix held. incident.io has now sat in 'Fixing' P3 status for 8 days with no substantive activity. The only new Slack event was a participant leaving the channel; incident.io's updated_at ticked forward but with no new status-update text behind it - looks administrative, not real progress.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: incident.io still stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status 8 days after the fix was confirmed working same day; the updated_at bump has no accompanying status update or closing note
Timeline (2)
  • 15 Jul 15:34 CEST a participant left the incident Slack channel (non-substantive)
  • incident.io updated_at ticked to 15 Jul 13:35 with no accompanying new status-update entry

→ Have Giancarlo Laferla post a short confirmation update and move status to Fixed/Closed now that a week-plus has passed with no recurrence.

INC-10810 slackP3open · evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

The substance is well in hand (fix landed, upgrade window scheduled with the partner) - purely a record-hygiene gap, but leaving someone on a month's leave listed as Incident Lead on an active P3 is a real handover risk.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload, a known daily 4pm choke pattern that finally got a real fix (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades over 3 weeks). Daniel Lindengren's 15 Jul handover to Sam Lundborg (RabbitMQ-upgrade coordination/cutover) was properly executed in Slack, but incident.io still lists Daniel (on leave until 10 Aug) as Incident Lead and status is unchanged at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul. No new activity this cycle beyond what was already captured.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status stuck 'Fixing' since 10 Jul and Incident Lead field still shows Daniel Lindengren (on leave until 10 Aug) - the real handover happened in Slack but hasn't been reflected in the record
Proposed / pending
  • RDS/cluster upgrade maintenance window scheduled for next Wednesday (~1am FT time) with the partner
  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination now owned by Sam Lundborg
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed
Timeline (1)
  • No new messages this cycle; incident.io roles/status unchanged

→ Sam Lundborg to formally reassign Incident Lead to himself, referencing the 15 Jul Slack handover, ahead of the scheduled maintenance window next Wednesday.

INC-13889 slack intercomP4open · evidence to 15 Jul 18:03 UTC
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

Low real risk (no player impact, PRs in flight) but this is squarely the silent-closure pattern - an unowned record plus an unanswered auto-resolve prompt is exactly how a P4 gets bot-closed without confirming the PRs shipped.

12Play's Chinese comms profile appeared missing from the backoffice - a display bug only, no player-facing impact. Rewards team opened two PRs 14 Jul; Daniel posted a detailed handover to Sam before vacation; no Incident Lead role has ever been formally assigned (only Reporter). New: the bot posted its stale-incident resolve-nudge at 20:03 CEST 15 Jul, still unanswered (~6h). No new Intercom activity.

Open breaches
  • D15/A1: incident.io still has no summary set and no formally assigned Incident Lead despite a detailed Slack handover, and the bot's 15 Jul auto-resolve nudge is now sitting unanswered
Proposed / pending
  • backoffice PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • rewards-backend PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • handoff to Sam Lundborg to verify both PRs
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix release tomorrow/Thursday - not yet confirmed done
Timeline (1)
  • 15 Jul 20:03 CEST incident.io auto-resolve nudge posted, still unanswered

→ Sam Lundborg to formally take the Incident Lead role, add a one-line summary, and confirm the two PRs' merge status before letting the bot resolve this.

INC-10938 slackP4open 20d
Security incident: Malicious dashboard creation attempt

private — review directly

🟢 Under control 3

3 handled well: fresh all-brands CRM/WorkOS outage (14303) escalated and communicated fast despite being P1; fresh Star Casino NL bonus-distributor alert (14301) has a lead, too fresh to flag; Data Science message-spike incident (14284) unchanged since this morning, still fresh.

INC-14303 slackP1open 2h· evidence to 16 Jul 08:38 UTC
All CRM Log In Not Available

Fast, correct handling of a fresh all-brands P1: instant acknowledgment, root cause found in ~1 minute, CTO engaged immediately, partner-facing comms out within 18 minutes (well under the C9 threshold) - too fresh and too well-handled to flag. Watch for full recovery confirmation and a closing update.

CRM login unavailable across all brands due to a WorkOS (third-party auth provider) outage. Joachim acknowledged and started investigating within seconds; root cause (WorkOS-side, confirmed via their status page and the authorize-URL timeout) identified within ~1 minute. Patrik joined immediately; an Intercom banner comms went out to affected users ~18 minutes after the alert. As of this check the outage is only partially recovered (a few brands not on WorkOS, e.g. Leonbets, are unaffected; others still very slow).

Proposed / pending
  • Wait on WorkOS to recover (external dependency) - in progress
  • Intercom banner comms to affected users - done, ~18min after alert
Timeline (6)
  • 16 Jul 08:13:03 CEST alert fires, Joachim acknowledges instantly
  • 08:14 CEST Joachim identifies the failing WorkOS authorize URL and timeout
  • 08:15-08:16 CEST Patrik confirms WorkOS-side (their status page), team floods in
  • 08:17 CEST comms plan agreed: Intercom banner to logged-in and logged-out users
  • 08:31 CEST banner live
  • 08:30-08:35 CEST mixed reports: some brands (not on WorkOS, e.g. Leonbets) unaffected, others still very slow/failing
INC-14284P3open 5h
Daily Data Science Message Spikes

Daily message spikes causing slow Data Science processing during 7-8:30AM and 7-8:45PM windows, triggering recurring alerts. Very fresh (~40min old at check time), lead already assigned, still Investigating.

INC-14301P3open 2h
Star Casino NL: queue star-casino-nl.actions.bonusdistributor is processing slow (messages: 25904, in: 0/s, delivery: 0/s)

Star Casino NL bonus-distributor queue processing slow (25,904 messages backlogged). Lead assigned (Giancarlo Laferla), accepted within 20 minutes. Very fresh (~33min old at check time) - not selected for full deep-read (P3, has a lead, no other trigger criteria met).
